author: @Caspar0s
text: Duogen is a wind/water generator for sailboats - goes in the water when sailing or anchored in moving water and the air when anchored.

author: @stewartpalmer2456
text: If you combine this design with video 1470 and design the spoons to be capacitive, you could get extra charge from the water as well. Just a thought.

author: @MrDWhit
text:
Sorry if this a daft question, 
Is it possible to have two forms of generation feeding into one battery?

Like, In theory could you paint the spoons with conductive  ink and add the gubbings of the rain drop generater on to each spoon and and have them both generating at the same time.

Damn that is super cool! I love your motivational spirit and can-do attitude. I think you're channeling your inner Victor Schoenberg....maybe I messed up the last name but I think you know whom I'm speaking of.
Have a blessed day brother, here's to you and yours.

author: @gordonlawrence1448
text: I was thinking that could work completely submerged with a toothed belt to the actual generator as you would get water flow over all spoons at the same time increasing torque. My "make it up as you go along" maths seems to indicate possibly 10W for 1m/s of flow.

replies.author: @UnifiedInfo
replies.text: If you completely submerge it youll get drag that will counter the force you want I assume you mean submerge maybe 1/3

author: @keeperofthegood
text: you just put the thought of a rain barrel in my head.   Lots of others but the rain barrel one could be interesting.  Instead of collecting water just to put in a barrel, along the way drive the spoons.  Setting it up 'scientifically' a funnel 617.8cm across and 250cm deep (1 meter square), to a 25mm feed pipe, and let it rain and drive, how much power can you harvest from a square meter of rain? (yes there are multiple factors but it begins with the idea :P)

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I agree mate - it always begins with an idea and to my mind it is the creativity of the idea that matters
